chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-2764 of 2025 ------------------------------------------------------------------- Date: Order with signature(s) of the Judge(s) ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 1. For orders on Misc. No.12510 of 2025 (urgency) 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on Misc. No.12511 of 2025. (exemption) 4. For hearing of main case. 04.07.2025. Mr. Fayyaz Hussain, Advocate is present along with the petitioners. Mr. K.W. Waswani and Mr. Ali Hyder Saleem, APG Sindh are present along with SIP/SHO Asad Ilahi of P.S. Shahdad Kot, B. Section Shahdadkot and I.O. ASI Sanam Kalhoro is also present. Mr. Raza Miana, DSP on behalf of I.G. Sindh is present. SHO P.S. ‘B” Section Shahdadkot is present and has filed a statement which is taken on record. Para wise comments have also been filed by Senior Superintendent of Police Kamber Shahdadkot, which are also taken on record. Investigating Officer of the FIR No.36 of 2023 registered under Sections 365-B, 457, 34 PPC of P.S. B. Section Shahdadkot is present and submits that previously she has filed report under Section 173 Cr.P.C. for disposal of the case under ‘A’ Class. The Investigating Officer further submits that now she has recorded statement of Mst. Sumaira/Petitioner No.1 under Section 161 Cr.P.C. wherein the petitioner No.1 had denied all the allegations leveled in the FIR. The Investigating Officer next submits that after conducting the investigation she is of the view that the case now shall be disposed of under ‘C’ class and she shall file such report before the Court having jurisdiction in the matter. In view of the above, statement of the Investigating Officer, counsel for the petitioner being satisfied seeks disposal of the instant petition,

The Investigating Officer next submits that after conducting the investigation she is of the view that the case now shall be disposed of under ‘C’ class and she shall file such report before the Court having jurisdiction in the matter. In view of the above, statement of the Investigating Officer, counsel for the petitioner being satisfied seeks disposal of the instant petition, however, submits that the official respondents may be directed to provide protection to the petitioners. This petition is accordingly disposed of with directions to the Investigating Officer to file the report under Section 173 Cr.P.C. before the Court having jurisdiction for disposal of the FIR under ‘C’ Class (as suggested by her) within 15 days. Official respondents are directed to provide protection to the petitioners in accordance with law as and when required. JUDGE JUDGE Farooq PS/.
